cases.name: γ_EM (Euler-Mascheroni constant)
cases.why_not_algebraic: Limit of harmonic series Σ(1/k − ln(k)) is NOT known to be algebraic or transcendental — trajectory-undecidable
cases.substrate_status: Substrate can compute partial sums at integer cycle granularity, but the LIMIT as a single number is not substrate-internal — it requires the limiting procedure, which is meta to the substrate cycles
cases.casey_universality_impact: γ_EM is a limit of substrate-computable quantities, not a substrate-internal identity. Substrate computes the trajectory; classification of the limit is meta-level.

cases.name: NS smooth solutions in 3D
cases.why_not_algebraic: Continuum-limit solutions to Navier-Stokes are functions on continuous space-time; algebraic-identity at substrate level applies to the L^∞ bound, not to the function value at every point
cases.substrate_status: Substrate represents NS at discrete commitment cycles; smooth continuum solution emerges as limit
cases.casey_universality_impact: Substrate observables (L^∞ bound, blow-up criterion) are algebraic-identity; the underlying continuous function is the limit of substrate trajectories

cases.name: 6 master integrals (math-frontier irreducible)
cases.source_toy: Mentioned in CLAUDE.md as math-frontier open
cases.why_not_algebraic: These are genuinely OPEN integrals in math — not known to reduce to known constants. Not BST-specific; math-frontier itself.
cases.substrate_status: Substrate may evaluate them numerically at high precision, but no closed-form algebraic identity is known
cases.casey_universality_impact: Not BST gap — open math question. If math eventually finds closed form, substrate may identify; otherwise, irreducible.

cases.name: Polynomial-time arithmetic complexity at large n
cases.why_not_algebraic: Computational complexity classes are statements about asymptotic resource use, not about individual quantities
cases.substrate_status: Substrate-derivable in terms of BST primary structure (Casey Curvature Principle); but the CLASSES themselves are not point identities
cases.casey_universality_impact: Substrate captures the asymptotic STRUCTURE algebraically (depth ≤ 1, P!=NP via curvature); individual class members are not point identities

cases.name: Probabilistic distributions (Bell, Born=Bergman)
cases.why_not_algebraic: Substrate computes probability distributions exactly; the OUTCOMES are sampled. Sampling introduces randomness, not algebra.
cases.substrate_status: Probability distribution IS algebraic-identity (per K67); individual outcomes are samples
cases.casey_universality_impact: Substrate distribution = algebraic-identity. Observed outcomes are statistical, but the underlying probability structure is exact.

cases.name: Anti-periodic boundary phase (JW fermion ring)
cases.why_not_algebraic: Fermionic Jordan-Wigner on a ring introduces anti-periodic boundary phase from anti-commutation; algebraic-identity on the cyclic structure requires explicit BC choice
cases.substrate_status: Substrate-internal: choosing periodic vs anti-periodic BC is a substrate-algebraic structural decision, not a continuum artifact
cases.casey_universality_impact: Algebraic-identity holds CONDITIONAL on BC choice. Substrate may have specific BC selection rule pending derivation.

universality_verdict: algebraic-identity holds for substrate-internal point quantities; meta-structures (limit, continuum, sampling, BC) are not point identities but are part of substrate operation
register_per_cal_flag_1: substrate IS algebraic-identity at point-quantity level; INTERFACES to observation involve meta-structures
